rt2-5-1.title: Hands-on: Expose a casting film fit for grading
rt2-5-1.intro: Castings are the most heavily radiographed product form, and grading their film against reference-radiograph sets like E446 only counts when density lands in the window. Set the gamma exposure for the casting shot, then keep the campaign alive by compensating for source decay.

tasks.label: Land the casting film inside the D 2.0-3.0 window
tasks.hint: Exposure E = A x t - lengthen the time until the density readout climbs into the window.

tasks.label: Press '30 days later' and re-expose to compensate
tasks.hint: Ir-192 halves every 73.8 days, so 30 days cuts activity to about 75% - stretch the exposure time by roughly a third.

rt2-5-2.title: Hands-on: Magnetize a rolled bar to catch its seam
rt2-5-2.intro: Rolling strings every surviving discontinuity out along the bar axis, and a magnetic field only finds a crack it crosses - the same orientation rule that hides laminations from a straight radiographic beam. Energize the head shot and the coil shot to reveal both flaws in this rolled bar, then lock in which field finds which.

tasks.label: Reveal the longitudinal seam left by rolling
tasks.hint: A head shot drives current down the bar - its circular flux crosses flaws that run with the axis.

tasks.label: Reveal the transverse crack
tasks.hint: A coil shot wraps the bar in longitudinal flux, which crosses flaws lying across the axis.

tasks.label: Answer the which-field-finds-which check
tasks.hint: Flux must cross a crack to leak - each field finds the flaw perpendicular to it.

rt2-5-3.title: Hands-on: Hunt sidewall fusion flaws the WPS predicts
rt2-5-3.intro: The WPS says short-circuiting GMAW, so the suspect list starts with lack of sidewall fusion along the groove faces - tight planar flaws film can miss unless the beam lines up. Sweep the 60-degree shear beam along the weld and mark all three hidden fusion-face flaws.

tasks.label: Mark the shallow fusion-face flaw
tasks.hint: Slide the wedge until the A-scan echo peaks, then press Mark within +/-4 mm of the peak.

tasks.label: Mark the mid-wall sidewall flaw
tasks.hint: On a first-leg beam, a deeper flaw peaks with the wedge standing farther back from it.

tasks.label: Mark the deep flaw near the root
tasks.hint: Keep sweeping the full scan line - the deepest hole echoes at the longest sound path.
